summary: High‑energy Alpine Valley with a blistering Dear Mr. Fantasy
blurb: The show stays tight and fun through the first set, then launches into an epic second set highlighted by Bird Song → Let It Grow and a scorching Dear Mr. Fantasy → The Other One sequence. Jerry’s soaring guitar and Brent’s psychedelic keys keep the energy sky‑high.
review: July 6, 1984 at Alpine Valley captures the Dead in top form. The first set rolls along with crisp renditions of classics like Iko Iko and a seamless Bird Song into Let It Grow that many reviewers called “crystal‑clear.” The second half is where the night truly soars: a blistering Dear Mr. Fantasy that launches into an intense The Other One, followed by a heartfelt Black Peter and a playful Why Don’t We Do It In The Road jam before closing with Sugar Magnolia and a triumphant Touch of Grey encore. Jerry’s guitar work on Fantasy is described as soaring and his vocals punchy, while Phil “bombs all over” the low end despite some recording‑related muting. Brent’s keyboards add trippy color throughout, and Bill’s drumming rides the jams from tight grooves to spacey passages, with Mickey holding steady behind the scenes. Across the various tapes the performance is consistently praised, making this a must‑listen Alpine Valley show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five out of seven recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly highlighting the Dear Mr. Fantasy performance and overall high energy; one mixed review notes fresh playing after a day off, while a single negative zero‑star review offers no substantive criticism. The weight of praise yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with crystal‑clear Bird Song/Let It Grow and a scorching Dear Mr. Fantasy; overall good balance despite slightly tinny mids.

key_highlights: Blistering Dear Mr. Fantasy → The Other One sequence that lifts the entire show
Seamless Bird Song into Let It Grow transition captured with surprising clarity on an audience tape
High‑energy first set and a lively second set jammed through Black Peter and Why Don’t We Do It In The Road
Strong keyboard textures from Brent and dynamic drumming that drive the extended jams

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring guitar on Dear Mr. Fantasy and powerful vocals early set; fresh, feeling‑it playing after a day off.
band_performance.Phil: Bass drives the band forward (“bombs all over”), though some recording muting slightly dulls low end.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm work with praised vocal on Why Don’t We Do It In The Road; keeps the groove steady throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Trippy, psychedelic keyboards add color; early‑set squawky tones improve later in the night.
band_performance.Billy: Dynamic drumming that moves from rocking grooves to spacey passages, underpinning the long jams.

must_listen_sequences: Why Don't We Do It In The Road
